items.title: 'Besiktas wil hoofdprijs voor Kökcu, Atlético bezig voor de zomer'
items.summary: We zitten in een periode tussen twee transferwindows, maar de stroom aan transfernieuws houdt nooit op. Traditiegetrouw volgt Voetbal International de buitenlandse markt op de voet. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: Arsenal exactly where we want to be - Arteta
items.summary: Mikel Arteta says Arsenal are "exactly where they want to be" in every competition as the Gunners get ready to face rivals Tottenham after the disappointing draw at Wolves.

items.title: Kompany: Mourinho made 'huge mistake' over Vini. Jr.
items.summary: Bayern Munich head coach jose Vincent Kompany has said Benfica boss José Mourinho made a "huge mistake" in his post-match comments following alleged racist abuse of Real Madrid forward Vinícius Júnior.

items.title: Pep Guardiola 'couldn't care less' about PL table
items.summary: Pep Guardiola insisted he "could not care less" about the tightening of the Premier League title race.

items.title: José on Vini, Madrid fallout: 'Hasn't been easy'
items.summary: José Mourinho admits it has been difficult to cope with the fallout from "every aspect" of Benfica's defeat to Real Madrid.

items.title: Sources: USMNT's Sargent set to join Toronto FC
items.summary: Toronto FC is in the final stages of acquiring United States international and Norwich City forward Josh Sargent, sources confirmed to ESPN.

items.title: Menzies reveals he has 'lost feeling in fingers' after punching table
items.summary: Cameron Menzies revealed he has "lost feeling in my fingers" after punching a table following defeat to Charlie Manby at the World Darts Championship.

items.title: Been gunt NEC Europees ticket: 'In 2008 was iedereen een maand dronken'
items.summary: Mario Been hoopt dat NEC komend seizoen Europees voetbal haalt. De oud-trainer van de Nijmegenaren zou het zelfs niet erg vinden als dat ten koste gaat van Feyenoord, zijn club.

items.title: Tudor 100% sure Spurs will stay up, Ratcliffe avoids FA action and more – live
items.summary: ⚽ Latest news, previews and updates before the weekend ⚽ 10 things to look out for | And email Tim Eddie Howe had some words of praise for Pep Guardiola and Manchester City: ““Genuinely, I think we learn something more about ourselves and our game going forward every time we play them. They have been the benchmark for a number of years for many teams. Each painful defeat we suffer at the Etihad we try to grow from it, evolve and improve. “They have been very, very good and are led by an outstanding manager. They continue to be the benchmark in my opinion.” Continue reading...

items.title: German soccer club cancel US trip amid concerns over ICE actions in Minnesota
items.summary: Werder Bremen scrap plans to play in Minnesota, Detroit Club cites unrest after ICE killings, US visa restrictions German soccer club Werder Bremen have canceled a trip to the United States due to concerns over unrest in Minnesota after the actions of US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) as well as economic risks, a club spokesperson told Reuters on Friday. The top-flight team were planning to visit Minnesota and Detroit in May and play two friendly matches, according to media reports in the US and Germany. No opponents for the matches had been confirmed. Continue reading...

items.title: Van Persie: 'Op de training schoot hij hem naast...'
items.summary: Casper Tengstedt schoot vorig weekend de winnende goal binnen vanaf de strafschopstip tegen Go Ahead Eagles. De Deen was niet de aangewezen man , maar benutte de penalty wel. Robin van Persie zag dat zijn pupil deze week op een soortgelijk moment het buitenkansje liet liggen op de training, maar is desondanks tevreden over Tengstedt.

items.title: Leclerc sets blistering pace for Ferrari on final day of F1 testing
items.summary: Charles Leclerc topped the final day of Formula 1 pre-season testing with a blistering lap to underline Ferrari's status as genuine contenders for the opening races of 2026.

items.title: Tudor '100%' convinced Spurs will avoid relegation
items.summary: New Tottenham head coach Igor Tudor says he is "100%" convinced Spurs will avoid relegation and still be in the Premier League next season.

items.title: Danilo met NEC terug bij Ajax: 'Wilde per se naar Nederland'
items.summary: Danilo staat met NEC voor een bijzondere wedstrijd. De 26-jarige spits treft met zijn nieuwe werkgever NEC zaterdag zijn oude club Ajax. De Amsterdammers haalden hem in 2017 naar Nederland, nu is hij terug. De Braziliaan blikt vooruit op het weerzien met Ajax en vertelt over zijn keuze voor NEC.

items.title: Carrick bespreekt voortgang van revaliderende De Ligt
items.summary: Manchester United heeft al anderhalve week niet gespeeld, en de volgende wedstrijd bij Everton staat pas voor maandagavond gepland. Manager Michael Carrick verscheen vrijdag wel voor de persconferentie, en bracht een hoopvolle update over Matthijs de Ligt.

items.title: Mourinho made 'unacceptable, huge mistake' - Kompany
items.summary: Bayern Munich boss Vincent Kompany says Jose Mourinho made a "huge mistake" with his "unacceptable" post-match comments after the alleged racial abuse of Real Madrid winger Vinicius Jr.

items.title: Ratcliffe avoids FA charge over immigration comments
items.summary: Manchester United co-owner Sir Jim Ratcliffe's claims the UK has been "colonised by immigrants" will not lead to any sanction by the Football Association.

items.title: Crystal Palace consider sacking Oliver Glasner after manager’s ‘not good enough’ remark
items.summary: Fans turned on Glasner during game on Thursday Assistant Paddy MacCarthy in line to step up if needed Crystal Palace are considering sacking Oliver Glasner after his latest public outburst in which the manager said he was “just not good enough” to turn around the club’s fortunes. Glasner has announced he will leave Palace at the end of the season but it is understood the chair, Steve Parish, could bring forward the Austrian’s departure after supporters turned on the manager during Thursday’s 1-1 draw at Zrinjski Mostar in the Conference League. It is unclear whether Glasner will remain in charge beyond Sunday’s game at home to Wolves, with his assistant Paddy MacCarthy in line to step in as a caretaker if needed. Continue reading...

items.title: Arteta: We can show what we're made of against Spurs
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has encouraged Arsenal to "show what they are made of" in Sunday's north London derby against Spurs as they aim to bounce back from their draw with Wolves.

items.title: Glasner's Crystal Palace future in serious doubt
items.summary: Sources tell BBC Sport that Crystal Palace's board is considering Oliver Glasner's future at the club.
